_comment: Strands ETL Slack App configuration. Secrets (signing_secret, bot_token) are stored in AWS Secrets Manager — never hardcode them here.

app.name: Strands ETL Agent
app.description: Slack interface for the Strands ETL validation, log analysis, and NL query agents

secrets._setup_note: Create secrets via: aws secretsmanager create-secret --name strands-etl/slack-signing-secret --secret-string '<value>'

/etl-analyze.description: Run AI agent analyzer on recent audit_validation failures

/etl-logs.description: Analyze Glue / Athena / Lambda / EMR logs for errors

/etl-query.description: Ask any question about your data in plain English (NL → Athena)
examples: /etl-query which clubs have the most failures this week?
/etl-query show me z-score anomalies for club CA-001 last 60 days
/etl-query count of failed records per pipeline today
/etl-query what is the average AI confidence score for true failures?

/etl-status.description: Show the last analyzer run summary

/etl-holiday.description: Check if a date is a known US holiday and see expected volume multipliers

/etl-help.description: Show all available ETL agent commands

slack_app_setup_steps: 1. Go to https://api.slack.com/apps → Create New App → From Scratch
2. App Name: 'Strands ETL Agent'  |  Workspace: your workspace
3. Basic Information → Signing Secret → copy → store in Secrets Manager as strands-etl/slack-signing-secret
4. OAuth & Permissions → Bot Token Scopes → Add: chat:write, commands, app_mentions:read
5. OAuth & Permissions → Install to Workspace → copy Bot User OAuth Token → store as strands-etl/slack-bot-token
6. Slash Commands → Create New Command for each of the 6 commands above
7. Deploy via SAM: cd slack && sam build && sam deploy --guided
8. Paste the API Gateway URL into each Slash Command's Request URL
9. Enable Event Subscriptions (optional) for @mentions: same API GW URL
10. Reinstall the app to your workspace after saving all settings
